'language': 'ja'} [[JA:和爾賀波神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11417869}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= 和爾賀波神社 |画像= [[File:Wanikawa Shrine, Ido.jpg|270px|和爾賀波神社]] |所在地= {{jalink|香川県|Q161454}}{{jalink|木田郡|Q1155929}}{{jalink|三木町|Q1205980}}大字井戸字熊田1582番地 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|豊玉姫命}} |社格= {{jalink|式内小社|Q134917287}}論社・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建= 不詳 |本殿= {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名= |札所= |例祭= 10月第1日曜日 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=和爾賀波
神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''和爾賀波神社'''（わにかわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|香川県|Q161454}}{{jalink|木田郡|Q1155929}}{{jalink|三木町|Q1205980}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|讃岐国|Q929682}}{{jalink|三木郡|Q10866143}}の{{jalink|式内社}}和爾賀波神社の{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}のひとつである。 三木町には同じ「わにかわじんじゃ」と呼ぶ神社として{{jalink|鰐河神社|Q11674320}}があり、こちらも式内和爾賀波神社の論社である論社として他に三木町下高岡の{{jalink|白山神社 (三木町)|Q11579589|白山神社}}と同町井上の{{jalink|引宮神社}}がある。。 === 祭神 === === 主祭神 === *{{jalink|トヨタマヒメ|Q2329261|豊玉比売命}} === 配祀 === *{{jalink|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|Q3082751|玉依比売命}} *{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997|八幡大神}} *{{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803|息長足姫命}} === 由緒 === 創建時期は不明。社伝によると、豊玉比売命が{{jalink|サメ|Q7372|鰐}}に乗って川を遡り、この地に上陸したという。その川は鰐川（{{jalink|鴨部川|Q11675608}}）という。 {{jalink|平安時代|Q193292|平安}}から{{jalink|室町時代|Q334845}}にかけて数回の{{jalink|遷宮|Q2271023}}の記録がある。式内和爾賀波神社の論社は4社あるが、この数多くの遷宮の影響があると推測される。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}となる。 === その他 === *豊玉比売命が鰐に乗って遡った川は後に鰐川となり、現在の{{jalink|鴨部川|Q11675608}}のことという式内和爾賀波神社の他の論社である鰐河、白山の両神社は、鰐川は{{jalink|新川 (香川県)|Q11502062|新川}}のことであるとしている。 ===交通=== *{{jalink|高松琴平電気鉄道|Q566998}}{{jalink|高松琴平電気鉄道長尾線|Q5364831|長尾線}}{{jalink|公文明駅|Q11392051}}より徒歩20分。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]]

'language': 'ja'} [[JA:和爾賀波神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11417869}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= 和爾賀波神社 |画像= [[File:Wanikawa Shrine, Ido.jpg|270px|和爾賀波神社]] |所在地= {{jalink|香川県|Q161454}}{{jalink|木田郡|Q1155929}}{{jalink|三木町|Q1205980}}大字井戸字熊田1582番地 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|豊玉姫命}} |社格= {{jalink|式内小社|Q134917287}}論社・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建= 不詳 |本殿= {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名= |札所= |例祭= 10月第1日曜日 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=和爾賀波
神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''和爾賀波神社'''（わにかわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|香川県|Q161454}}{{jalink|木田郡|Q1155929}}{{jalink|三木町|Q1205980}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|讃岐国|Q929682}}{{jalink|三木郡|Q10866143}}の{{jalink|式内社}}和爾賀波神社の{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}のひとつである。 三木町には同じ「わにかわじんじゃ」と呼ぶ神社として{{jalink|鰐河神社|Q11674320}}があり、こちらも式内和爾賀波神社の論社である論社として他に三木町下高岡の{{jalink|白山神社 (三木町)|Q11579589|白山神社}}と同町井上の{{jalink|引宮神社}}がある。。 === 祭神 === === 主祭神 === *{{jalink|トヨタマヒメ|Q2329261|豊玉比売命}} === 配祀 === *{{jalink|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|Q3082751|玉依比売命}} *{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997|八幡大神}} *{{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803|息長足姫命}} === 由緒 === 創建時期は不明。社伝によると、豊玉比売命が{{jalink|サメ|Q7372|鰐}}に乗って川を遡り、この地に上陸したという。その川は鰐川（{{jalink|鴨部川|Q11675608}}）という。 {{jalink|平安時代|Q193292|平安}}から{{jalink|室町時代|Q334845}}にかけて数回の{{jalink|遷宮|Q2271023}}の記録がある。式内和爾賀波神社の論社は4社あるが、この数多くの遷宮の影響があると推測される。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}となる。 === その他 === *豊玉比売命が鰐に乗って遡った川は後に鰐川となり、現在の{{jalink|鴨部川|Q11675608}}のことという式内和爾賀波神社の他の論社である鰐河、白山の両神社は、鰐川は{{jalink|新川 (香川県)|Q11502062|新川}}のことであるとしている。 ===交通=== *{{jalink|高松琴平電気鉄道|Q566998}}{{jalink|高松琴平電気鉄道長尾線|Q5364831|長尾線}}{{jalink|公文明駅|Q11392051}}より徒歩20分。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''和爾賀波神社'''（わにかわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|香川県|Q161454}}{{jalink|木田郡|Q1155929}}{{jalink|三木町|Q1205980}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|讃岐国|Q929682}}{{jalink|三木郡|Q10866143}}の{{jalink|式内社}}和爾賀波神社の{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}のひとつである。 三木町には同じ「わにかわじんじゃ」と呼ぶ神社として{{jalink|鰐河神社|Q11674320}}があり、こちらも式内和爾賀波神社の論社である論社として他に三木町下高岡の{{jalink|白山神社 (三木町)|Q11579589|白山神社}}と同町井上の{{jalink|引宮神社}}がある。。 === 祭神 === === 主祭神 === *{{jalink|トヨタマヒメ|Q2329261|豊玉比売命}} === 配祀 === *{{jalink|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|Q3082751|玉依比売命}} *{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997|八幡大神}} *{{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803|息長足姫命}} === 由緒 === 創建時期は不明。社伝によると、豊玉比売命が{{jalink|サメ|Q7372|鰐}}に乗って川を遡り、この地に上陸したという。その川は鰐川（{{jalink|鴨部川|Q11675608}}）という。 {{jalink|平安時代|Q193292|平安}}から{{jalink|室町時代|Q334845}}にかけて数回の{{jalink|遷宮|Q2271023}}の記録がある。式内和爾賀波神社の論社は4社あるが、この数多くの遷宮の影響があると推測される。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}となる。 === その他 === *豊玉比売命が鰐に乗って遡った川は後に鰐川となり、現在の{{jalink|鴨部川|Q11675608}}のことという式内和爾賀波神社の他の論社である鰐河、白山の両神社は、鰐川は{{jalink|新川 (香川県)|Q11502062|新川}}のことであるとしている。 ===交通=== *{{jalink|高松琴平電気鉄道|Q566998}}{{jalink|高松琴平電気鉄道長尾線|Q5364831|長尾線}}{{jalink|公文明駅|Q11392051}}より徒歩20分。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]

'language': 'ja'} [[JA:和爾賀波神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11417869}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= 和爾賀波神社 |画像= [[File:Wanikawa Shrine, Ido.jpg|270px|和爾賀波神社]] |所在地= {{jalink|香川県|Q161454}}{{jalink|木田郡|Q1155929}}{{jalink|三木町|Q1205980}}大字井戸字熊田1582番地 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|豊玉姫命}} |社格= {{jalink|式内小社|Q134917287}}論社・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建= 不詳 |本殿= {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名= |札所= |例祭= 10月第1日曜日 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=和爾賀波
神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''和爾賀波神社'''（わにかわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|香川県|Q161454}}{{jalink|木田郡|Q1155929}}{{jalink|三木町|Q1205980}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|讃岐国|Q929682}}{{jalink|三木郡|Q10866143}}の{{jalink|式内社}}和爾賀波神社の{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}のひとつである。 三木町には同じ「わにかわじんじゃ」と呼ぶ神社として{{jalink|鰐河神社|Q11674320}}があり、こちらも式内和爾賀波神社の論社である論社として他に三木町下高岡の{{jalink|白山神社 (三木町)|Q11579589|白山神社}}と同町井上の{{jalink|引宮神社}}がある。。 === 祭神 === === 主祭神 === *{{jalink|トヨタマヒメ|Q2329261|豊玉比売命}} === 配祀 === *{{jalink|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|Q3082751|玉依比売命}} *{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997|八幡大神}} *{{jalink|神功皇后|Q232803|息長足姫命}} === 由緒 === 創建時期は不明。社伝によると、豊玉比売命が{{jalink|サメ|Q7372|鰐}}に乗って川を遡り、この地に上陸したという。その川は鰐川（{{jalink|鴨部川|Q11675608}}）という。 {{jalink|平安時代|Q193292|平安}}から{{jalink|室町時代|Q334845}}にかけて数回の{{jalink|遷宮|Q2271023}}の記録がある。式内和爾賀波神社の論社は4社あるが、この数多くの遷宮の影響があると推測される。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}となる。 === その他 === *豊玉比売命が鰐に乗って遡った川は後に鰐川となり、現在の{{jalink|鴨部川|Q11675608}}のことという式内和爾賀波神社の他の論社である鰐河、白山の両神社は、鰐川は{{jalink|新川 (香川県)|Q11502062|新川}}のことであるとしている。 ===交通=== *{{jalink|高松琴平電気鉄道|Q566998}}{{jalink|高松琴平電気鉄道長尾線|Q5364831|長尾線}}{{jalink|公文明駅|Q11392051}}より徒歩20分。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QWanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QWanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}

'language': 'ja'} [[JA:和爾賀波神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11417869}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Wanikawa Shrine |画像= [[File:Wanikawa Shrine, Ido.jpg|270px|Wanikawa Shrine]] |所在地= {{jalink|Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454}}{{jalink|Kita District|Q1155929}}{{jalink|Miki|Q1205980}} Ōaza Ido, Aza Kumada 1582 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto}} |社格= {{jalink|Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287}} candidate shrine・former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名= |札所= |例祭= First Sunday of October |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Wanikawa
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Wanikawa Shrine''' (わにかわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Miki|Q1205980}}, {{jalink|Kita District|Q1155929}}, {{jalink|Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454}}. It is one of the {{jalink|candidate shrines|Q135022904}} for Wanikawa Shrine, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Miki District|Q10866143}}, {{jalink|Sanuki Province|Q929682}}. In Miki there is also {{jalink|Wanigawa Shrine|Q11674320}}, likewise read “wanikawa jinja,” and that shrine is also a candidate for the same ShikinaishaOther candidate shrines include {{jalink|Hakusan Shrine (Miki)|Q11579589|Hakusan Shrine}} in Shimotakaoka, Miki, and {{jalink|Hikimiya Shrine}} in Inoue, Miki.. === Enshrined deities === === Main deity === *{{jalink|Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto}} === Associated deities === *{{jalink|Tamayori-hime (Hyūga mythology)|Q3082751|Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|Hachiman Ōkami}} *{{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto}} === Origin === The founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto rode a {{jalink|shark|Q7372|wani}} upstream and landed here. That river is called Wani River ({{jalink|Kamobe River|Q11675608}}). There are records of several {{jalink|sengū (shrine relocations)|Q2271023}} from the {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292|Heian}} through the {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. There are four candidate shrines for the Shikinaisha Wanikawa Shrine, and this is presumed to reflect those many relocations. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), it became a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. === Other === *The river Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto ascended on a wani later came to be called Wani River, said to be today’s {{jalink|Kamobe River|Q11675608}}The other candidate shrines for Wanikawa Shikinaisha, Wanigawa and Hakusan Shrines, identify Wani River with {{jalink|Shinkawa (Kagawa)|Q11502062|Shinkawa}}. ===Access=== *20 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Kumonmyō Station|Q11392051}} on the {{jalink|Nagao Line|Q5364831}} of {{jalink|Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Q566998}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QWanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QWanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}

'language': 'ja'} [[JA:和爾賀波神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11417869}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Wanikawa Shrine |画像= [[File:Wanikawa Shrine, Ido.jpg|270px|Wanikawa Shrine]] |所在地= {{jalink|Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454}}{{jalink|Kita District|Q1155929}}{{jalink|Miki|Q1205980}} Ōaza Ido, Aza Kumada 1582 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto}} |社格= {{jalink|Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287}} candidate shrine・former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名= |札所= |例祭= First Sunday of October |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Wanikawa
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Wanikawa Shrine''' (わにかわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Miki|Q1205980}}, {{jalink|Kita District|Q1155929}}, {{jalink|Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454}}. It is one of the {{jalink|candidate shrines|Q135022904}} for Wanikawa Shrine, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Miki District|Q10866143}}, {{jalink|Sanuki Province|Q929682}}. In Miki there is also {{jalink|Wanigawa Shrine|Q11674320}}, likewise read “wanikawa jinja,” and that shrine is also a candidate for the same ShikinaishaOther candidate shrines include {{jalink|Hakusan Shrine (Miki)|Q11579589|Hakusan Shrine}} in Shimotakaoka, Miki, and {{jalink|Hikimiya Shrine}} in Inoue, Miki.. === Enshrined deities === === Main deity === *{{jalink|Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto}} === Associated deities === *{{jalink|Tamayori-hime (Hyūga mythology)|Q3082751|Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|Hachiman Ōkami}} *{{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto}} === Origin === The founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto rode a {{jalink|shark|Q7372|wani}} upstream and landed here. That river is called Wani River ({{jalink|Kamobe River|Q11675608}}). There are records of several {{jalink|sengū (shrine relocations)|Q2271023}} from the {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292|Heian}} through the {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. There are four candidate shrines for the Shikinaisha Wanikawa Shrine, and this is presumed to reflect those many relocations. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), it became a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. === Other === *The river Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto ascended on a wani later came to be called Wani River, said to be today’s {{jalink|Kamobe River|Q11675608}}The other candidate shrines for Wanikawa Shikinaisha, Wanigawa and Hakusan Shrines, identify Wani River with {{jalink|Shinkawa (Kagawa)|Q11502062|Shinkawa}}. ===Access=== *20 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Kumonmyō Station|Q11392051}} on the {{jalink|Nagao Line|Q5364831}} of {{jalink|Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Q566998}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QWanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QWanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}

Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Wanikawa Shrine''' (わにかわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Miki|Q1205980}}, {{jalink|Kita District|Q1155929}}, {{jalink|Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454}}. It is one of the {{jalink|candidate shrines|Q135022904}} for Wanikawa Shrine, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Miki District|Q10866143}}, {{jalink|Sanuki Province|Q929682}}. In Miki there is also {{jalink|Wanigawa Shrine|Q11674320}}, likewise read “wanikawa jinja,” and that shrine is also a candidate for the same ShikinaishaOther candidate shrines include {{jalink|Hakusan Shrine (Miki)|Q11579589|Hakusan Shrine}} in Shimotakaoka, Miki, and {{jalink|Hikimiya Shrine}} in Inoue, Miki.. === Enshrined deities === === Main deity === *{{jalink|Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto}} === Associated deities === *{{jalink|Tamayori-hime (Hyūga mythology)|Q3082751|Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto}} *{{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|Hachiman Ōkami}} *{{jalink|Empress Jingū|Q232803|Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto}} === Origin === The founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto rode a {{jalink|shark|Q7372|wani}} upstream and landed here. That river is called Wani River ({{jalink|Kamobe River|Q11675608}}). There are records of several {{jalink|sengū (shrine relocations)|Q2271023}} from the {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292|Heian}} through the {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}. There are four candidate shrines for the Shikinaisha Wanikawa Shrine, and this is presumed to reflect those many relocations. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), it became a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. === Other === *The river Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto ascended on a wani later came to be called Wani River, said to be today’s {{jalink|Kamobe River|Q11675608}}The other candidate shrines for Wanikawa Shikinaisha, Wanigawa and Hakusan Shrines, identify Wani River with {{jalink|Shinkawa (Kagawa)|Q11502062|Shinkawa}}. ===Access=== *20 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Kumonmyō Station|Q11392051}} on the {{jalink|Nagao Line|Q5364831}} of {{jalink|Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Q566998}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QWanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QWanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}[[Category:qq]]

Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454}}{{ill|lt=Kita District|Q1155929|WD=Q1155929}}{{ill|lt=Miki|Q1205980|WD=Q1205980}} Ōaza Ido, Aza Kumada 1582 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} |社格= {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}} candidate shrine・former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名= |札所= |例祭= First Sunday of October |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Wanikawa
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Wanikawa Shrine''' (わにかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Miki|Q1205980|WD=Q1205980}}, {{ill|lt=Kita District|Q1155929|WD=Q1155929}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=candidate shrines|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} for Wanikawa Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Miki District|Q10866143|WD=Q10866143}}, {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682}}. In Miki there is also {{ill|lt=Wanigawa Shrine|Q11674320|WD=Q11674320}}, likewise read “wanikawa jinja,” and that shrine is also a candidate for the same ShikinaishaOther candidate shrines include {{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine (Miki)|Q11579589|WD=Q11579589|lt=Hakusan Shrine}} in Shimotakaoka, Miki, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hikimiya Shrine|UNKNOWN}} in Inoue, Miki.. === Enshrined deities === === Main deity === *{{ill|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto}} === Associated deities === *{{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime (Hyūga mythology)|Q3082751|WD=Q3082751|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hachiman Ōkami}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto}} === Origin === The founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto rode a {{ill|lt=shark|Q7372|WD=Q7372|lt=wani}} upstream and landed here. That river is called Wani River ({{ill|lt=Kamobe River|Q11675608|WD=Q11675608}}). There are records of several {{ill|lt=sengū (shrine relocations)|Q2271023|WD=Q2271023}} from the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian}} through the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}}. There are four candidate shrines for the Shikinaisha Wanikawa Shrine, and this is presumed to reflect those many relocations. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), it became a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Other === *The river Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto ascended on a wani later came to be called Wani River, said to be today’s {{ill|lt=Kamobe River|Q11675608|WD=Q11675608}}The other candidate shrines for Wanikawa Shikinaisha, Wanigawa and Hakusan Shrines, identify Wani River with {{ill|lt=Shinkawa (Kagawa)|Q11502062|WD=Q11502062|lt=Shinkawa}}. ===Access=== *20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kumonmyō Station|Q11392051|WD=Q11392051}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagao Line|Q5364831|WD=Q5364831}} of {{ill|lt=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Q566998|WD=Q566998}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QWanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QWanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

|本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名= |札所= |例祭= First Sunday of October |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Wanikawa
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Wanikawa Shrine''' (わにかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Miki|Q1205980|WD=Q1205980}}, {{ill|lt=Kita District|Q1155929|WD=Q1155929}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=candidate shrines|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} for Wanikawa Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Miki District|Q10866143|WD=Q10866143}}, {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682}}. In Miki there is also {{ill|lt=Wanigawa Shrine|Q11674320|WD=Q11674320}}, likewise read “wanikawa jinja,” and that shrine is also a candidate for the same ShikinaishaOther candidate shrines include {{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine (Miki)|Q11579589|WD=Q11579589|lt=Hakusan Shrine}} in Shimotakaoka, Miki, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hikimiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Inoue, Miki.. === Enshrined deities === === Main deity === *{{ill|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto}} === Associated deities === *{{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime (Hyūga mythology)|Q3082751|WD=Q3082751|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hachiman Ōkami}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto}} === Origin === The founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto rode a {{ill|lt=shark|Q7372|WD=Q7372|lt=wani}} upstream and landed here. That river is called Wani River ({{ill|lt=Kamobe River|Q11675608|WD=Q11675608}}). There are records of several {{ill|lt=sengū (shrine relocations)|Q2271023|WD=Q2271023}} from the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian}} through the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}}. There are four candidate shrines for the Shikinaisha Wanikawa Shrine, and this is presumed to reflect those many relocations. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), it became a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Other === *The river Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto ascended on a wani later came to be called Wani River, said to be today’s {{ill|lt=Kamobe River|Q11675608|WD=Q11675608}}The other candidate shrines for Wanikawa Shikinaisha, Wanigawa and Hakusan Shrines, identify Wani River with {{ill|lt=Shinkawa (Kagawa)|Q11502062|WD=Q11502062|lt=Shinkawa}}. ===Access=== *20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kumonmyō Station|Q11392051|WD=Q11392051}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagao Line|Q5364831|WD=Q5364831}} of {{ill|lt=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Q566998|WD=Q566998}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QWanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QWanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), it became a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Other === *The river Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto ascended on a wani later came to be called Wani River, said to be today’s {{ill|lt=Kamobe River|Q11675608|WD=Q11675608}}The other candidate shrines for Wanikawa Shikinaisha, Wanigawa and Hakusan Shrines, identify Wani River with {{ill|lt=Shinkawa (Kagawa)|Q11502062|WD=Q11502062|lt=Shinkawa}}. ===Access=== *20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kumonmyō Station|Q11392051|WD=Q11392051}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagao Line|Q5364831|WD=Q5364831}} of {{ill|lt=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Q566998|WD=Q566998}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QWanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QWanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[JA:和爾賀波神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11417869}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Wanikawa Shrine |画像= [[File:Wanikawa Shrine, Ido.jpg|270px|Wanikawa Shrine]] |所在地= {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kita District|Q1155929|WD=Q1155929|1=Kita District, Kagawa}}{{ill|lt=Miki|Q1205980|WD=Q1205980|1=Miki, Kagawa}} Ōaza Ido, Aza Kumada 1582 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} |社格= {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} candidate shrine・former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= |札所= |例祭= First Sunday of October |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Wanikawa
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Wanikawa Shrine''' (わにかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Miki|Q1205980|WD=Q1205980|1=Miki, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kita District|Q1155929|WD=Q1155929|1=Kita District, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=candidate shrines|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for Wanikawa Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Miki District|Q10866143|WD=Q10866143|1=Miki district}}, {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}. In Miki there is also {{ill|lt=Wanigawa Shrine|Q11674320|WD=Q11674320|1=Wanikawa Shrine}}, likewise read “wanikawa jinja,” and that shrine is also a candidate for the same ShikinaishaOther candidate shrines include {{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine (Miki)|Q11579589|WD=Q11579589|lt=Hakusan Shrine|1=Hakusan Shrine (Q11579589)}} in Shimotakaoka, Miki, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hikimiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Inoue, Miki.. === Enshrined deities === === Main deity === *{{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} === Associated deities === *{{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime (Hyūga mythology)|Q3082751|WD=Q3082751|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Emperor Ōjin}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} === Origin === The founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto rode a {{ill|lt=shark|Q7372|WD=Q7372|lt=wani|1=Shark}} upstream and landed here. That river is called Wani River ({{ill|lt=Kamobe River|Q11675608|WD=Q11675608|1=Kabe River}}). There are records of several {{ill|lt=sengū (shrine relocations)|Q2271023|WD=Q2271023|1=Sengū}} from the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian|1=Heian period}} through the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. There are four candidate shrines for the Shikinaisha Wanikawa Shrine, and this is presumed to reflect those many relocations. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it became a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Other === *The river Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto ascended on a wani later came to be called Wani River, said to be today’s {{ill|lt=Kamobe River|Q11675608|WD=Q11675608|1=Kabe River}}The other candidate shrines for Wanikawa Shikinaisha, Wanigawa and Hakusan Shrines, identify Wani River with {{ill|lt=Shinkawa (Kagawa)|Q11502062|WD=Q11502062|lt=Shinkawa|1=Shinkawa River}}. ===Access=== *20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kumonmyō Station|Q11392051|WD=Q11392051|1=Kumonmyō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagao Line|Q5364831|WD=Q5364831|1=Kotoden Nagao Line}} of {{ill|lt=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Q566998|WD=Q566998|1=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QWanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QWanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

In Miki there is also {{ill|lt=Wanigawa Shrine|Q11674320|WD=Q11674320|1=Wanikawa Shrine}}, likewise read “wanikawa jinja,” and that shrine is also a candidate for the same ShikinaishaOther candidate shrines include {{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine (Miki)|Q11579589|WD=Q11579589|lt=Hakusan Shrine|1=Hakusan Shrine (Q11579589)}} in Shimotakaoka, Miki, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hikimiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Inoue, Miki.. === Enshrined deities === === Main deity === *{{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} === Associated deities === *{{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime (Hyūga mythology)|Q3082751|WD=Q3082751|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Emperor Ōjin}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} === Origin === The founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto rode a {{ill|lt=shark|Q7372|WD=Q7372|lt=wani|1=Shark}} upstream and landed here. That river is called Wani River ({{ill|lt=Kamobe River|Q11675608|WD=Q11675608|1=Kabe River}}). There are records of several {{ill|lt=sengū (shrine relocations)|Q2271023|WD=Q2271023|1=Sengū}} from the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian|1=Heian period}} through the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. There are four candidate shrines for the Shikinaisha Wanikawa Shrine, and this is presumed to reflect those many relocations. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it became a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Other === *The river Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto ascended on a wani later came to be called Wani River, said to be today’s {{ill|lt=Kamobe River|Q11675608|WD=Q11675608|1=Kabe River}}The other candidate shrines for Wanikawa Shikinaisha, Wanigawa and Hakusan Shrines, identify Wani River with {{ill|lt=Shinkawa (Kagawa)|Q11502062|WD=Q11502062|lt=Shinkawa|1=Shinkawa River}}. ===Access=== *20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kumonmyō Station|Q11392051|WD=Q11392051|1=Kumonmyō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagao Line|Q5364831|WD=Q5364831|1=Kotoden Nagao Line}} of {{ill|lt=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Q566998|WD=Q566998|1=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QWanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QWanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it became a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Other === *The river Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto ascended on a wani later came to be called Wani River, said to be today’s {{ill|lt=Kamobe River|Q11675608|WD=Q11675608|1=Kabe River}}The other candidate shrines for Wanikawa Shikinaisha, Wanigawa and Hakusan Shrines, identify Wani River with {{ill|lt=Shinkawa (Kagawa)|Q11502062|WD=Q11502062|lt=Shinkawa|1=Shinkawa River}}. ===Access=== *20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kumonmyō Station|Q11392051|WD=Q11392051|1=Kumonmyō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagao Line|Q5364831|WD=Q5364831|1=Kotoden Nagao Line}} of {{ill|lt=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Q566998|WD=Q566998|1=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わにかわしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QWanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QWanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)}}[[Category:qq]]

|位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} |社格= {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} candidate shrine・former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= |札所= |例祭= First Sunday of October |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Wanikawa
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Wanikawa Shrine''' (わにかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Miki|Q1205980|WD=Q1205980|1=Miki, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kita District|Q1155929|WD=Q1155929|1=Kita District, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=candidate shrines|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for Wanikawa Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Miki District|Q10866143|WD=Q10866143|1=Miki district}}, {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}. In Miki there is also {{ill|lt=Wanigawa Shrine|Q11674320|WD=Q11674320|1=Wanikawa Shrine}}, likewise read “wanikawa jinja,” and that shrine is also a candidate for the same ShikinaishaOther candidate shrines include {{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine (Miki)|Q11579589|WD=Q11579589|lt=Hakusan Shrine|1=Hakusan Shrine (Q11579589)}} in Shimotakaoka, Miki, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hikimiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Inoue, Miki.. === Enshrined deities === === Main deity === *{{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} === Associated deities === *{{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime (Hyūga mythology)|Q3082751|WD=Q3082751|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Emperor Ōjin}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} === Origin === The founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto rode a {{ill|lt=shark|Q7372|WD=Q7372|lt=wani|1=Shark}} upstream and landed here. That river is called Wani River ({{ill|lt=Kamobe River|Q11675608|WD=Q11675608|1=Kabe River}}). There are records of several {{ill|lt=sengū (shrine relocations)|Q2271023|WD=Q2271023|1=Sengū}} from the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian|1=Heian period}} through the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. There are four candidate shrines for the Shikinaisha Wanikawa Shrine, and this is presumed to reflect those many relocations. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it became a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Other === *The river Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto ascended on a wani later came to be called Wani River, said to be today’s {{ill|lt=Kamobe River|Q11675608|WD=Q11675608|1=Kabe River}}The other candidate shrines for Wanikawa Shikinaisha, Wanigawa and Hakusan Shrines, identify Wani River with {{ill|lt=Shinkawa (Kagawa)|Q11502062|WD=Q11502062|lt=Shinkawa|1=Shinkawa River}}. ===Access=== *20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kumonmyō Station|Q11392051|WD=Q11392051|1=Kumonmyō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagao Line|Q5364831|WD=Q5364831|1=Kotoden Nagao Line}} of {{ill|lt=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Q566998|WD=Q566998|1=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]] [[Category:qq]]

|神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Wanikawa
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Wanikawa Shrine''' (わにかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Miki|Q1205980|WD=Q1205980|1=Miki, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kita District|Q1155929|WD=Q1155929|1=Kita District, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=candidate shrines|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for Wanikawa Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Miki District|Q10866143|WD=Q10866143|1=Miki district}}, {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}. In Miki there is also {{ill|lt=Wanigawa Shrine|Q11674320|WD=Q11674320|1=Wanikawa Shrine}}, likewise read “wanikawa jinja,” and that shrine is also a candidate for the same ShikinaishaOther candidate shrines include {{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine (Miki)|Q11579589|WD=Q11579589|lt=Hakusan Shrine|1=Hakusan Shrine (Q11579589)}} in Shimotakaoka, Miki, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hikimiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Inoue, Miki.. === Enshrined deities === === Main deity === *{{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} === Associated deities === *{{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime (Hyūga mythology)|Q3082751|WD=Q3082751|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Emperor Ōjin}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} === Origin === The founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto rode a {{ill|lt=shark|Q7372|WD=Q7372|lt=wani|1=Shark}} upstream and landed here. That river is called Wani River ({{ill|lt=Kamobe River|Q11675608|WD=Q11675608|1=Kabe River}}). There are records of several {{ill|lt=sengū (shrine relocations)|Q2271023|WD=Q2271023|1=Sengū}} from the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian|1=Heian period}} through the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. There are four candidate shrines for the Shikinaisha Wanikawa Shrine, and this is presumed to reflect those many relocations. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it became a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Other === *The river Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto ascended on a wani later came to be called Wani River, said to be today’s {{ill|lt=Kamobe River|Q11675608|WD=Q11675608|1=Kabe River}}The other candidate shrines for Wanikawa Shikinaisha, Wanigawa and Hakusan Shrines, identify Wani River with {{ill|lt=Shinkawa (Kagawa)|Q11502062|WD=Q11502062|lt=Shinkawa|1=Shinkawa River}}. ===Access=== *20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kumonmyō Station|Q11392051|WD=Q11392051|1=Kumonmyō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagao Line|Q5364831|WD=Q5364831|1=Kotoden Nagao Line}} of {{ill|lt=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Q566998|WD=Q566998|1=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:香川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]]

In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it became a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Other === *The river Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto ascended on a wani later came to be called Wani River, said to be today’s {{ill|lt=Kamobe River|Q11675608|WD=Q11675608|1=Kabe River}}The other candidate shrines for Wanikawa Shikinaisha, Wanigawa and Hakusan Shrines, identify Wani River with {{ill|lt=Shinkawa (Kagawa)|Q11502062|WD=Q11502062|lt=Shinkawa|1=Shinkawa River}}. ===Access=== *20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kumonmyō Station|Q11392051|WD=Q11392051|1=Kumonmyō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagao Line|Q5364831|WD=Q5364831|1=Kotoden Nagao Line}} of {{ill|lt=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Q566998|WD=Q566998|1=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]]

|位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} |社格= {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} candidate shrine・former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= |札所= |例祭= First Sunday of October |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Wanikawa
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Wanikawa Shrine''' (わにかわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Miki|Q1205980|WD=Q1205980|1=Miki, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kita District|Q1155929|WD=Q1155929|1=Kita District, Kagawa}}, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=candidate shrines|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for Wanikawa Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Miki District|Q10866143|WD=Q10866143|1=Miki district}}, {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}. In Miki there is also {{ill|lt=Wanigawa Shrine|Q11674320|WD=Q11674320|1=Wanikawa Shrine}}, likewise read “wanikawa jinja,” and that shrine is also a candidate for the same ShikinaishaOther candidate shrines include {{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine (Miki)|Q11579589|WD=Q11579589|lt=Hakusan Shrine|1=Hakusan Shrine (Q11579589)}} in Shimotakaoka, Miki, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hikimiya Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Inoue, Miki.. === Enshrined deities === === Main deity === *{{ill|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} === Associated deities === *{{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime (Hyūga mythology)|Q3082751|WD=Q3082751|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}} *{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Emperor Ōjin}} *{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|lt=Okinagatarashihime-no-Mikoto|1=Empress Jingū}} === Origin === The founding period is unknown. According to shrine tradition, Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto rode a {{ill|lt=shark|Q7372|WD=Q7372|lt=wani|1=Shark}} upstream and landed here. That river is called Wani River ({{ill|lt=Kamobe River|Q11675608|WD=Q11675608|1=Kabe River}}). There are records of several {{ill|lt=sengū (shrine relocations)|Q2271023|WD=Q2271023|1=Sengū}} from the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian|1=Heian period}} through the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. There are four candidate shrines for the Shikinaisha Wanikawa Shrine, and this is presumed to reflect those many relocations. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it became a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Other === *The river Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto ascended on a wani later came to be called Wani River, said to be today’s {{ill|lt=Kamobe River|Q11675608|WD=Q11675608|1=Kabe River}}The other candidate shrines for Wanikawa Shikinaisha, Wanigawa and Hakusan Shrines, identify Wani River with {{ill|lt=Shinkawa (Kagawa)|Q11502062|WD=Q11502062|lt=Shinkawa|1=Shinkawa River}}. ===Access=== *20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kumonmyō Station|Q11392051|WD=Q11392051|1=Kumonmyō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagao Line|Q5364831|WD=Q5364831|1=Kotoden Nagao Line}} of {{ill|lt=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Q566998|WD=Q566998|1=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]]

There are four candidate shrines for the Shikinaisha Wanikawa Shrine, and this is presumed to reflect those many relocations. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it became a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Other === *The river Toyotama-hime-no-Mikoto ascended on a wani later came to be called Wani River, said to be today’s {{ill|lt=Kamobe River|Q11675608|WD=Q11675608|1=Kabe River}}The other candidate shrines for Wanikawa Shikinaisha, Wanigawa and Hakusan Shrines, identify Wani River with {{ill|lt=Shinkawa (Kagawa)|Q11502062|WD=Q11502062|lt=Shinkawa|1=Shinkawa River}}. ===Access=== *20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kumonmyō Station|Q11392051|WD=Q11392051|1=Kumonmyō Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagao Line|Q5364831|WD=Q5364831|1=Kotoden Nagao Line}} of {{ill|lt=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad|Q566998|WD=Q566998|1=Takamatsu-Kotohira Electric Railroad}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wanikawa Shrine (Q11417869)]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:三木町の歴史]] [[Category:三木町の建築物]]
